title: chaerophyllum procumbens var. procumbens
intro: Chaerophyllum procumbens var. procumbens, also known as long-leaved chervil or lacy chervil, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Chaerophyllum procumbens var. procumbens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Chaerophyllum procumbens var. procumbens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
